abstract: Apply a new color scheme to your article.
overview:
## Overview

In this third advanced article design, you’ll create some visual effects using a dark color scheme, a `video` component, and a fixed image fill.

**On this page, you’ll learn how to use ComponentStyle objects to implement a color scheme**.



### Define a Dark Background Color for Components

You can use a dark color scheme to create contrast between one group of articles and another or for variety within a single article.

This `ComponentStyle` object has a dark background color.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/giving-the-article-a-dark-color-scheme#darkBackgroundStyle-Result].

#### darkBackgroundStyle: Copy This Code

#### darkBackgroundStyle: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Define a Light-Color Text Style

The `ComponentTextStyle` object called `bodyFirstDropCapDark` has a light text color.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/giving-the-article-a-dark-color-scheme#bodyFirstDropCapDark-Result].

#### bodyFirstDropCapDark: Copy This Code

#### bodyFirstDropCapDark: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Implement the Dark Color Scheme

You’ll move some components from the header into a new section and add references to a new `ComponentStyle` object and a new `ComponentTextStyle` object.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/giving-the-article-a-dark-color-scheme#Dark-Color-Scheme-Result].

After you make these changes in your code, you can preview your working `article.json` file in News Preview to see the dark color scheme.

#### Components: Copy This Code

#### Section Boundary: Copy This Code

#### Dark Color Scheme: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

### Set the Default to a Dark Color Scheme

To view the dark color scheme in Dark Mode, switch your simulator device to a dark systemwide appearance (Features > Toggle Appearance).

You can maintain the dark color scheme for your article even when a user switches between a light or dark appearance. Set the `preferredColorScheme` conditional to define the background color for a specific appearance. For more information about conditionals, see Apple News Format > [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Condition].

In [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/giving-the-article-a-dark-color-scheme#Define-a-Dark-Background-Color-for-Components], you created a new `ComponentStyle` called `darkBackgroundStyle`. Now you’ll update that style with a conditional to explicitly set a value for your article’s  dark appearance.

#### Condition Dark Mode: Copy This Code

#### Condition Dark Mode: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

To view the dark color scheme when the device appearance is toggled, preview your working `article.json` file in News Preview.

**Light Appearance**



**Dark Appearance**



### Further Exploration

For information about using Dark Mode and adjusting designs for light and dark appearances, see [https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/visual-design/dark-mode/].

For information about updating your article so that it adapts when Dark Mode is active, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/supporting-dark-mode-for-your-article].

### Previous

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/viewing-the-finished-article-for-advanced-design-tutorial-2]

### Next

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-a-video]

title: Giving the Article a Dark Color Scheme
